name: Niigata Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: passion, vitality and the rising sun
purity and snow, evoking Niigata's snowy winters

description: A red field charged at the centre with a white emblem combining the katakana ニ (ni) and a stylised abstraction of the kanji 潟 (kata).
history: Niigata's prefectural symbol was adopted in 1963; the flag, with the white emblem on a red field, has been used since the same year.

features.symbolism: a stylised abstraction of the kana ニ and the kanji 潟 (kata), the second character of Niigata; the design also evokes a snowflake-like form referencing the prefecture's heavy snowfall.
